scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
issuing_body: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment
eligibility_conditions: Indian national
Student with benchmark disability (40% or more disability) as per Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016
Studying in Class IX or X
Enrolled in a Government school or Government-recognized school
Regular, full-time student
Parental income not exceeding Rs. 2.5 lakh per annum
Valid Aadhaar card or Aadhaar enrollment
UDID or UDID enrollment number
Not more than 2 siblings already availing the scheme
Not availing any other scholarship simultaneously
required_documents: Valid disability certificate (40% or more) from competent authority
Aadhaar card or Aadhaar enrollment number
UDID card or UDID enrollment number
Income certificate of parent/guardian (from Revenue Officer)
Proof of age (birth certificate or school record)
office_to_visit: State Nodal Officer / Education or Welfare Department of the student's home state; Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, 5th Floor, B Wing, Pt. Deendayal Antyodaya Bhawan, CGO Complex, New Delhi-110003

scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
issuing_body: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment
eligibility_conditions: Indian national
Student with benchmark disability (40% or more disability) as per Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016
Pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary courses up to Master's degree level
Enrolled in recognized institution or university
Parental income not exceeding Rs. 2.5 lakh per annum
Valid Aadhaar card and biometric authentication
UDID or UDID enrollment number
Not availing any other scholarship simultaneously
Not more than 2 siblings availing the scheme
Correspondence/distance education students are also eligible
required_documents: Valid disability certificate (40% or more) from competent authority
Aadhaar card or Aadhaar enrollment number
UDID card or UDID enrollment number
office_to_visit: State Nodal Officer / Education or Welfare Department of the student's home state; Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, 5th Floor, B Wing, Pt. Deendayal Antyodaya Bhawan, CGO Complex, New Delhi-110003

scheme_name: Top Class Education Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
issuing_body: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment
eligibility_conditions: Indian national
Student with benchmark disability (40% or more disability)
Pursuing Graduate or Post-Graduate Degree/Diploma in notified Institutes of Excellence
Parental income not exceeding Rs. 8 lakh per annum
Valid Aadhaar and UDID
Only full-time courses eligible (no distance/part-time/weekend courses)
Award given only once per individual in a lifetime
Not availing any other scholarship simultaneously
required_documents: Proof of admission in notified institution
Bills/vouchers for aids and assistive devices
office_to_visit: State Nodal Officer / Education or Welfare Department; Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, 5th Floor, B Wing, Pt. Deendayal Antyodaya Bhawan, CGO Complex, New Delhi-110003

scheme_name: National Overseas Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities
issuing_body: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment
eligibility_conditions: Indian national
Student with benchmark disability (40% or more disability)
Below 35 years of age as on 1st April of selection year
55% marks or equivalent grade in qualifying degree
For PhD: 55% in relevant Master's degree
For Master's: 55% in relevant Bachelor's degree
Parental income not exceeding Rs. 8 lakh per annum
Valid Aadhaar and UDID
Unconditional or compatible conditional offer from top 500 QS-ranked foreign university
Not pursuing/completed Master's or PhD abroad with government scholarship previously
Award given only once in a lifetime
Not pursuing courses in Indian Culture/Heritage/History/Social Studies topics
required_documents: Surety bond on non-judicial stamp paper (Rs. 50,000)
office_to_visit: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, 5th Floor, B Wing, Pt. Deendayal Antyodaya Bhawan, CGO Complex, New Delhi-110003. Email: sandeepkumar.rth@nic.in

scheme_name: National Fellowship for Persons with Disabilities (NFPwD)
issuing_body: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD) via NDFDC
eligibility_conditions: Indian national
Student with benchmark disability (40% or more disability)
Pursuing regular and full-time M.Phil or PhD in UGC-recognized university
Qualified in UGC-NET JRF or UGC-CSIR NET-JRF test
No income ceiling
Not employed in any university/college/government institution (even on study leave)
Not availing any other scholarship or stipend
Fellowship can be availed only once in a lifetime
required_documents: UGC-NET JRF or CSIR-NET JRF score card
Admission letter from university for M.Phil/PhD
office_to_visit: National Divyangjan Finance and Development Corporation (NDFDC); Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, CGO Complex, New Delhi-110003

scheme_name: Free Coaching Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
issuing_body: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD)
eligibility_conditions: Indian national
Student with benchmark disability (40% or more disability)
Valid Aadhaar and UDID
Family income not exceeding Rs. 8 lakh per annum
Not availing benefit of any other coaching scheme from Centre or State Government
Not more than 2 siblings availing the scheme
Benefits can be availed only once
For Class XII-level exams: must have passed or be studying in Class XII
For bachelor-level exams: must have completed or be in final year of bachelor's degree
required_documents: Declaration of not availing other coaching scheme
office_to_visit: Implementing Agencies (National Institutes and Composite Regional Centres under DEPwD); Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, CGO Complex, New Delhi

scheme_name: Central Sector Scheme of Top Class Education for OBC, EBC and DNT Students
issuing_body: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
eligibility_conditions: Student belonging to OBC, EBC, or DNT category
Total annual family income from all sources not exceeding Rs. 2.5 lakh
Secured admission in a full-time course in a notified 'Top Class' institution
Admission must be based on general selection criteria of institution
Not more than 2 siblings availing benefit in a family
Must get promoted to next semester/class for renewal
Students from IITs, IIMs, IIITs, AIIMSs, NITs, NIFTs, NIDs, Hotel Management Institutes, National Law Universities eligible
For private institutions not in above list: must be NAAC A++ or A+ or Top 100 NIRF or Institution of National Importance
required_documents: OBC/EBC/DNT caste certificate issued by competent authority
Income certificate (annual family income up to Rs. 2.5 lakh)
Admission rank/merit proof for the respective entrance exam
Affidavit confirming not the third sibling availing the scheme
office_to_visit: Respective notified institution's scholarship coordinator; Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Shastri Bhawan, New Delhi

financial_assistance.tuition_fee: Full tuition fee (up to Rs. 2 lakh/year for private, full for government)

scheme_name: Ishan Uday Special Scholarship Scheme for North Eastern Region
eligibility_conditions: Student passed Class XII or equivalent from a school situated within North Eastern Region (NER)
Pursuing first UG Degree programme only
Admitted in first year of UG degree programme in eligible university/college in India
Parental income below Rs. 4.5 lakh per annum
Must have a valid Domicile Certificate of any NER state
Regular/Full Time mode only (no open/distance/correspondence/private/part-time)
Students admitted through Management Quota not eligible
Not availing any other scholarship simultaneously
Institution must have valid AISHE code and NAAC accreditation (from AY 2025-26)
required_documents: Class XII marksheet from NER school
Income certificate (parental income below Rs. 4.5 lakh)
office_to_visit: University Grants Commission, Selection & Awards Bureau, Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi-110002

scheme_name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students (Degree)
issuing_body: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
eligibility_conditions: Must be a girl student
Admitted to First year of Degree level course OR Second year through lateral entry in AICTE-approved institution
Maximum two girl children per family are eligible
Family income from all sources not more than Rs. 8 lakh per annum
Valid income certificate issued by State/UT Government required
Gap between qualifying exam and admission should not be more than two years
Not availing any other scholarship or financial assistance
Must not fail/drop out in subsequent years
required_documents: Income certificate issued by State/UT Government (family income up to Rs. 8 lakh)
Marksheet of qualifying exam (10+2 or equivalent)
Bank account details (in student's own name, KYC compliant)
office_to_visit: AICTE, Nelson Mandela Marg, Vasant Kunj, New Delhi-110070; Department of Technical Education of respective State/UT

financial_assistance.scholarship: Rs. 50,000/year for up to 4 years (first year entry) or 3 years (lateral entry)

scheme_name: PM-USP Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students (PM-USP CSSS)
issuing_body: Ministry of Education, Government of India
eligibility_conditions: Students above 80th percentile of successful candidates in relevant stream from respective Board in Class XII
Pursuing regular degree courses only (not correspondence, distance mode, or diploma courses)
Enrolled in colleges/institutions recognized by AICTE and respective regulatory bodies
Not availing any other scholarship or fee waiver/reimbursement scheme
Gross parental/family income up to Rs. 4.5 lakh per annum
Must maintain at least 50% marks in annual examination for renewal
Must maintain at least 75% attendance for renewal
No disciplinary/criminal complaints including ragging
Must apply online on National Scholarships Portal
required_documents: Class XII marksheet (above 80th percentile in Board exam)
Income certificate (parental/family income up to Rs. 4.5 lakh)
Disability certificate (for 5% horizontal reservation, if applicable)
Bank account details (student's own account for DBT)
Admission proof from institution with valid AISHE code
office_to_visit: National Scholarship Division, Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education, West Block 1, 2nd Floor, Wing 6, Room No. 6, R.K. Puram, Sector 1, New Delhi-110066. Tel: 011-20862360. Email: ns1-scholarship@gov.in
